name: Shared Infrastructure
description: Database schema, TypeScript types, auth config, layouts, constants, and shared UI components
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma schema migrates successfully and creates all tables (User, Account, Session, VerificationToken, Assessment, ApiKey, AssessmentEvent)
TypeScript types compile with no errors for all engine data structures
NextAuth config supports email/password and Google OAuth login
Root layout renders Header and Footer on all pages
Dashboard layout renders sidebar and redirects unauthenticated users to /auth/login
Tailwind CSS and all Radix UI primitives render correctly
Zod validation schemas are importable from lib/validation/schemas.ts
Rate limiter middleware is configured and functional
Environment variables are validated on startup

name: Risk Classification Questionnaire
description: 12-question branching questionnaire that classifies an AI system into unacceptable/high/limited/minimal risk
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user lands on /checker, When the page loads, Then they see Question 1 with no signup required
Given a user is on any question, When they select an answer, Then the next relevant question appears via branching logic
Given a user answers a question indicating a prohibited practice, When the questionnaire detects this, Then it short-circuits to Unacceptable Risk classification immediately with explanation
Given a user completes all applicable questions, When results are calculated, Then the classification is one of: Unacceptable, High, Limited, or Minimal risk
Given a user reaches the results page, When results display, Then the specific EU AI Act articles triggering that classification are cited
Given a user refreshes the page mid-questionnaire, When the page reloads, Then their progress is preserved via URL state or localStorage

name: Obligation Checklist
description: Display specific actionable obligation checklist based on risk classification and user role
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user is classified as High Risk, When the results page loads, Then they see a checklist with all applicable obligations including Art. 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 43, 49, 72
Given a user is classified as Limited Risk, When the results page loads, Then they see only transparency obligations (Art. 50) applicable to their system type
Given a user is classified as Minimal Risk, When the results page loads, Then they see No mandatory obligations with a recommendation to follow voluntary codes of conduct
Given a user is classified as Unacceptable Risk, When the results page loads, Then they see a clear warning that this AI practice is prohibited with the specific Article 5 provision cited
Given any obligation item, When the user clicks it, Then an expandable section shows a plain-language explanation, the exact article reference, and a what this means in practice summary
Given a user is a deployer not provider, When obligations display, Then only deployer-specific obligations are shown

name: Shareable Compliance Badge
description: Unique URL that renders an SVG badge showing the AI system's risk classification level
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user completes an assessment, When results display, Then a badge preview is shown with the classification level and date
Given a user clicks Copy Badge URL, When the URL is copied, Then it points to /api/badge/[assessmentId] which returns an SVG image
Given a badge URL is requested, When the server responds, Then it returns a valid SVG with risk level color-coded, assessment date, and EU AI Act label
Given the badge is embedded in an external site via img tag, When rendered, Then it displays correctly at any size with crisp text
Given a user has not created an account, When they generate a badge, Then the badge uses an anonymous assessment ID stored in a cookie or localStorage with a 90-day expiration
Badge colors are: Unacceptable red #DC2626, High orange #EA580C, Limited yellow #CA8A04, Minimal green #16A34A

name: Results Summary & PDF Export
description: Export the full assessment as a downloadable PDF with classification, obligations, and article references
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user is on the results page, When they click Download PDF, Then a PDF is generated and downloaded containing system description, risk classification with reasoning, full obligation checklist, applicable article references, and assessment date
Given the PDF is generated, When opened, Then it includes a header with EU AI Act Compliance Assessment and a footer with the tool URL and disclaimer
Given the PDF is generated, When reviewed, Then it contains a disclaimer stating this is an informational tool and not legal advice

name: Landing Page & SEO
description: Marketing landing page with hero, features, FAQ, deadline countdown, and SEO optimization
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user visits /, When the page loads, Then they see a headline communicating the value prop, a Start Assessment CTA above the fold, a brief explanation of the EU AI Act with key deadlines, and social proof
Given search engine crawlers index the page, When /robots.txt and /sitemap.xml are requested, Then they return valid responses including all public pages
Given a user visits /, When they view the page, Then the meta title is EU AI Act Compliance Checker — Free Risk Classification Tool
Given a user on mobile visits /, When the page loads, Then all content is readable and the CTA is tappable without horizontal scrolling

name: Assessment Persistence API
description: API routes to create, read, list, update, and delete assessments in the database
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user completes an assessment, When POST /api/assessments is called, Then the assessment is stored in the database and returned with an ID
Given an authenticated user, When GET /api/assessments is called, Then all their assessments are returned sorted by updatedAt descending
Given a valid assessment ID, When GET /api/assessments/[id] is called, Then the full assessment is returned if the caller is the owner or anonymous ID matches
Given an authenticated user, When DELETE /api/assessments/[id] is called, Then the assessment is deleted if they are the owner
Given an authenticated user, When PATCH /api/assessments/[id] is called, Then the assessment name or emailReminders can be updated

name: Documentation Templates
description: Downloadable Markdown templates for required compliance documentation based on risk classification
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user is classified as High Risk, When they view the results page, Then they see a Documentation Templates section with templates for Technical Documentation, Risk Management Plan, Data Governance Policy, Human Oversight Protocol, and Post-Market Monitoring Plan
Given a user clicks a template, When it downloads, Then it contains structured sections with placeholder text, guidance comments, and references to the relevant articles
Given a user is classified as Limited Risk, When they view templates, Then only the Transparency Disclosure template is offered
Given a user is classified as Minimal Risk, When they view templates, Then no templates are shown and only voluntary code of conduct guidance appears

name: User Accounts & Saved Assessments
description: Optional account creation to save, manage, and track multiple AI system assessments over time
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user completes an assessment without an account, When they click Save Assessment, Then they are prompted to create an account via email/password or Google OAuth
Given a user creates an account, When they log in, Then they see a dashboard listing all their saved assessments with system name, classification, date, and last updated
Given a user clicks an existing assessment, When the detail page loads, Then they can view the full results, re-take the questionnaire, or delete the assessment
Given a user has multiple assessments, When they view the dashboard, Then assessments are sorted by last updated date newest first
Given a user deletes their account, When the deletion completes, Then all their data is permanently removed within 30 days

name: Assessment Comparison View
description: Side-by-side comparison of multiple assessments to prioritize compliance efforts
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user has 2+ saved assessments, When they select 2-4 assessments and click Compare, Then a comparison table displays showing system name, risk level, applicable obligations with checkmarks, and key differences highlighted
Given the comparison view is displayed, When the user views it, Then obligations present in one assessment but not another are visually highlighted

name: Compliance Timeline
description: Visual timeline showing when each obligation becomes enforceable with countdown to deadlines
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user views their results, When they click View Timeline, Then a visual timeline shows key dates with the user's relevant dates highlighted
Given the current date is before a deadline, When the timeline renders, Then the upcoming deadline shows a countdown in days remaining
Given the current date is past a deadline, When the timeline renders, Then that deadline shows In effect with a checkmark

name: Email Deadline Reminders
description: Opt-in email notifications sent at 90, 60, 30, and 7 days before compliance deadlines
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user has an account and a saved assessment, When they toggle Email Reminders on, Then they receive emails at 90, 60, 30, and 7 days before each applicable deadline
Given a user receives a reminder email, When they click the link, Then they are taken to their assessment results page
Given a user toggles reminders off, When the toggle is saved, Then no further emails are sent

name: Multi-Language Support
description: Questionnaire and results available in English, German, and French
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user visits the site, When they click the language selector, Then they can choose English, German, or French
Given a user selects German, When the questionnaire loads, Then all questions, answers, results, and obligation descriptions are displayed in German
Given a user switches language mid-assessment, When the language changes, Then their progress is preserved and the current question re-renders in the new language

name: Public API Access
description: Public REST API for programmatic access to the classification engine with API key authentication
acceptanceCriteria: Given a developer sends a POST request to /api/v1/classify with valid answers JSON, When the server processes it, Then it returns a JSON response with risk_level, applicable_obligations, cited_articles, and badge_url
Given a developer sends invalid or incomplete answers, When the server validates, Then it returns a 422 error with specific field-level validation messages
Given the API is called without an API key, When rate limiting is checked, Then anonymous requests are limited to 10/hour per IP
Given a developer has an account, When they generate an API key from their dashboard, Then authenticated requests are limited to 100/hour

name: Analytics Events
description: Track assessment events (started, completed, abandoned, pdf_downloaded, badge_copied) for success metrics
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user starts an assessment, When the first question loads, Then a started event is recorded
Given a user completes an assessment, When results display, Then a completed event is recorded
Given a user downloads a PDF, When the download succeeds, Then a pdf_downloaded event is recorded
Given a user copies a badge URL, When the copy succeeds, Then a badge_copied event is recorded
